# 🔄 Pull Request

## 📋 Description

Brief description of what this PR does and why it's needed.

## 🔧 Type of Change

Please delete options that are not relevant:

- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] 📝 Documentation update
- [ ] 🎨 Style/UI changes
- [ ] ♻️ Code refactoring (no functional changes)
- [ ] ⚡ Performance improvements
- [ ] 🧪 Test additions or modifications
- [ ] 🔧 Build/CI changes

## 🔗 Related Issues

Closes #(issue number)
Fixes #(issue number)
Related to #(issue number)

## ✅ Checklist

Please check all that apply:

### Code Quality
- [ ] My code follows the style guidelines of this project
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes

### Documentation
- [ ] I have updated the README.md if needed
- [ ] I have updated relevant documentation in the `docs/` folder
- [ ] I have added/updated API documentation if applicable
- [ ] I have updated the CHANGELOG.md

### Database Changes (if applicable)
- [ ] I have created necessary database migrations
- [ ] I have updated the database documentation
- [ ] I have tested migrations on a clean database
- [ ] I have considered backward compatibility

### Frontend Changes (if applicable)
- [ ] Components are responsive and work on mobile devices
- [ ] I have tested in multiple browsers
- [ ] Accessibility guidelines have been followed
- [ ] Performance impact has been considered

### Backend Changes (if applicable)
- [ ] API endpoints are properly documented
- [ ] Input validation is implemented
- [ ] Error handling is appropriate
- [ ] Security considerations have been addressed
